headlines: U.S. Treasury opens public comment on GENIUS stablecoin rules
Coinbase wins conditional OCC national trust-charter approval
Charles Schwab says 'Schwab Crypto' accounts are coming soon
Major Solana DEX Drift suffers $270–$285M exploit
Marathon (MARA) sells $1.1B in BTC, cuts staff as it pivots to AI/data centers

articles.title: Treasury seeks public comment on GENIUS stablecoin rules
articles.summary: The U.S. Treasury published a notice of proposed rulemaking under the GENIUS Act that would set standards for state-level oversight of payment stablecoin issuers and seeks public feedback. The move aims to align state rules with new federal stablecoin guardrails.

articles.title: Coinbase receives conditional approval for a national trust charter from the OCC
articles.summary: Coinbase said U.S. banking regulators gave conditional approval for a national trust company charter for Coinbase National Trust Co., a step that could place custody and market‑infrastructure business under federal supervision and boost institutional trust. Coinbase stressed it does not plan to take retail deposits or become a traditional bank.

articles.title: Charles Schwab teases 'Schwab Crypto'—direct Bitcoin & Ether trading coming
articles.summary: Charles Schwab added a 'Crypto at Schwab' page and an interest list saying 'Schwab Crypto' accounts to buy and sell Bitcoin and Ethereum are coming soon for most U.S. states, signaling major retail brokerage entry into spot crypto trading.
articles.source: Charles Schwab official page / industry reports

articles.title: Drift Protocol (Solana) hit by $270–$285M exploit
articles.summary: Drift, a large Solana-based perpetuals DEX, was exploited in a major attack that drained roughly $270–$285 million. Protocol teams paused activity and are coordinating with analytics firms and exchanges to track and contain stolen funds — a big security shock for Solana DeFi.

articles.title: MARA sells 15,133 BTC for $1.1B and cuts ~15% of staff to fund AI pivot
articles.summary: Marathon (MARA) disclosed it sold about 15,133 BTC (~$1.1 billion) in March to reduce convertible debt, and announced workforce cuts as it repositions mining sites toward AI and data‑center services. The move highlights miners selling BTC to shore up balance sheets and pursue new revenue lines.

articles.title: Riot Platforms sold 3,778 BTC in Q1 amid miner sell‑offs
articles.summary: Riot Platforms disclosed Q1 sales of 3,778 BTC (roughly $289.5M at reported averages) as listed miners continued to liquidate inventory during a weak quarter for mining economics. The broader miner sell‑off is a key driver of short‑term Bitcoin supply pressure.

articles.title: Metaplanet bought 5,075 BTC in Q1 to become the 3rd‑largest corporate Bitcoin treasury
articles.summary: Tokyo‑listed Metaplanet disclosed a large Q1 accumulation of 5,075 BTC (bringing its total to ~40,177 BTC), underscoring that several public companies keep adding to bitcoin treasuries even as prices correct.

articles.title: Linux Foundation, Coinbase and Big Tech back x402 Foundation for agentic‑AI payments
articles.summary: The Linux Foundation launched the x402 Foundation to standardize agentic‑AI payment rails. Google, Microsoft, AWS, Visa, Mastercard, Coinbase and others signaled support — a development at the crossroads of crypto payments, AI agents, and traditional finance infrastructure.
